solution:
def hammingWeight(self, n: int) -> int:
    count = 0
    while n > 0:
        count += n & 1
        n //= 2
    return count

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log(n))
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW

Explanation:
The problem requires counting the number of set bits in the binary representation of the input number. We can solve this problem using a while loop that iterates log(n) times, where n is the input number. In each iteration, we check the least significant bit (LSB) of n using the bitwise AND operator (&) and increment the count by 1. Then, we clear the LSB by performing integer division by 2 (//=). This approach has a time complexity of O(log(n)). The space complexity is O(1) because we only use a single variable (count) to store the result.
